title: 控方证人
summary:
Following a conversation with a woman who expresses a sense of being separated by time, McPherson observes the devoted love between Morris Hovells and his wife, Alice, during lunch. After their meeting, the woman suggests they may never meet again. The next morning, McAllister recalls a previous incident involving a car and a sudden fog. He visits the residence of Miss Alastair Haversham, only to be met by a grieving housekeeper. She informs him that Miss Haversham has died after a captain mistakenly gave her the wrong bottle of tonic in the dark. McAllister views her body in a room illuminated by red light from stained glass. Later, at a different location, McPherson speaks with a landlady, Mrs. Rose, regarding local legends of ghosts, such as sailors and gypsies. A young woman named Rachel arrives to see him, and he greets her with a kiss.

In a separate setting, Lady Lancaster inspects number 19, an ancient and desolate house in Westminster. Despite the house's gloomy atmosphere and low rent, the practical Lady Lancaster insists on discovering why it has remained vacant. The house agent, Mr. Radish, reluctantly reveals that the vacancy is linked to a child. He explains that thirty years prior, a man named Williams lived there with his sickly son. Williams was later identified as an escaped convict and committed suicide rather than surrendering. The young boy remained alone in the house, obeying his father's orders never to leave, while neighbors heard him crying in the deserted building at night.
